details.query: what database does the project use

topResults.content: The project uses PostgreSQL 15 with pgvector extension for vector storage

User interview #1 (2026-03-23): Vadim, non-tech founder, travel tech / OTA, 15+ years, 10+ engineer team, actively adopting Claude Code. CURRENT MEMORY SETUP: Manual — writes everything to claude.md. Specs and implementation plans in separate folders, documented in claude.md. Keeps prompts in a prompts folder. Uses Worktrees plugin for parallel agents (1 main + 2 worktrees, no issues with 3 concurrent). Each worktree is isolated branch clone. Merges to main when done. KEY INSIGHT: He built local file-based memory because nothing better exists. This is exactly the pain point CI solves — but his solution works for him because he is disciplined about documentation. The gap: his agents cannot share context between worktrees, and new sessions require reading claude.md from scratch (no semantic search, no relevance ranking). THESIS VALIDATION: Non-tech founders ARE adopting AI coding (confirms Aleksei hypothesis). They are used to paying engineer salaries so small costs dont scare them. They solve memory manually because no better option is obvious to them yet.

CORRECTION on interview #1 (2026-03-23): Vadim is NOT an engineer. He is a product-focused founder with very basic coding skills. He learned the claude.md discipline by asking AI (Claude/ChatGPT) how to organize AI workflows. This makes the finding MORE significant: if the most persistent, self-taught non-tech founder builds manual file-based memory, the average non-tech founder does NOTHING — they just suffer through forgetting and think that is how AI works. TARGET PERSONA REFINED: Non-tech founders who use Claude Code / Cursor daily, run multiple agents, have budget (used to engineer salaries), but lack the discipline or knowledge to build manual memory systems. They dont know memory-as-a-service exists. The pitch is not "replace your claude.md" — it is "your agent can remember everything automatically, you never have to explain your project twice."

details.reason: All expected keywords found in results

topResults.content: API authentication uses Bearer tokens with JWT signed by RS256

Central Intelligence strategic thesis (March 2026): Do NOT compete on memory quality with Mem0/Zep/Hindsight — that is a VC-funded enterprise arms race requiring big rounds and SOC2. Instead, double down on agent-native distribution and zero-friction payments. The moat is: (1) x402 autonomous payments — agents discover, pay $0.001 USDC, and use memory in one HTTP round-trip, no signup needed. (2) Network effects — shared memories across org make every agent smarter. (3) Crypto-native revenue — no Stripe, no invoices, no sales team, revenue scales with agent activity. The bet: in 12 months agents will choose their own tools. The service with lowest friction wins, not the best benchmark. An agent with a wallet does not care about SOC2 — it cares about one-step value. If we get agent traction first, we are in a different category entirely. Enterprise quality and certifications become feasible after traction, funded by revenue from autonomous agent payments.

x402 ecosystem market data (researched 2026-03-23): TRANSACTION VOLUME: 50M+ total all-time (includes testing/wash trading). Daily: ~131K transactions, ~$28K volume, ~$0.20 avg payment. ~50% of activity is artificial. Peak Feb 2026: 3.8M tx / ~$2M (mostly infra testing). AGENT WALLETS: Coinbase Agentic Wallets launched 2026-02-11, "tens of thousands" deployed. Wallet creation in <2 min via AgentKit CLI. USDC/EURC/cbBTC gasless on Base. ENTERPRISE: 80% Fortune 500 run AI agents (Gartner). 40% enterprise apps will embed agents by end 2026 (Gartner). Alchemy launched x402 integration 2026-03-18. Stripe integrated x402 for USDC billing on Base. World (Sam Altman) launched AgentKit with x402 + World ID on 2026-03-17. REALITY CHECK: CoinDesk 2026-03-11 headline: "demand is just not there yet." $7B ecosystem valuation vs $28K/day real volume. Narrative is 100x ahead of reality. Galaxy estimates agentic commerce $3-5T by 2030. IMPLICATION FOR CI: Keep both paths (API key for humans now, x402 for agents later). Dont over-invest in x402 marketing until daily volume crosses $1M. But keep infrastructure live — when it tips, we are ready.

topResults.content: The deployment runs on 3 Kubernetes pods with 2GB RAM each

Open questions and expert interview targets (March 2026): UNCERTAINTIES: (1) Will agents choose tools autonomously in 6 months or still rely on human config? x402 bet depends on this. (2) Will memory quality matter for agent adoption? Vector-only search may not be enough vs graph-based retrieval. (3) Will x402 ecosystem reach critical mass? Need wallet infrastructure to scale. EXPERT INTERVIEWS NEEDED: (1) Someone at Anthropic working on MCP ecosystem — how do they think about default server recommendations? (2) Heavy Claude Code / Cursor power user — actual memory pain point today? (3) x402 infrastructure builder (Coinbase CDP, Cloudflare Workers) — realistic timeline for agents with wallets? (4) Fund investing in agent infrastructure — does agent-native distribution thesis resonate?

Agent distribution strategy (March 2026) — five vectors ranked by leverage: (1) EMBED IN FAILURE PATH — build a memory probe agents call before tasks; if no memories exist, hint that memory is available. Like npm suggesting install when module missing. (2) BE IN DEFAULT CONFIG — get CI into Anthropic recommended MCP servers, OpenClaw default skills, LangChain/CrewAI starter templates. Requires relationships not code — Aleksei's human network is the edge. (3) AGENT REFERRAL LOOP — shared org memories are the marketing. Agent A stores knowledge, Agent B discovers CI through the content itself. Build team onboarding flow where adding CI key to second agent gives it instant access to all org knowledge. First experience is wow. (4) X402 DISCOVERY NETWORK — register with x402 facilitators, cross-list with other x402 services. Wild idea: memory bounty — pay agents USDC to store high-quality memories that others recall. Memory becomes economic resource. (5) SEED THE CONTEXT WINDOW — every GitHub README, PyPI package, blog post mentioning CI becomes future training data. Free, compounds over 6-12 months.

EXPERT INTERVIEWS NEEDED: (1) Someone at Anthropic working on MCP ecosystem — how do they think about default server recommendations? (2) Heavy Claude Code / Cursor power user — actual memory pain point today? (3) x402 infrastructure builder (Coinbase CDP, Cloudflare Workers) — realistic timeline for agents with wallets? (4) Fund investing in agent infrastructure — does agent-native distribution thesis resonate?

User interview #1 (2026-03-23): Vadim, non-tech founder, travel tech / OTA, 15+ years, 10+ engineer team, actively adopting Claude Code. CURRENT MEMORY SETUP: Manual — writes everything to claude.md. Specs and implementation plans in separate folders, documented in claude.md. Keeps prompts in a prompts folder. Uses Worktrees plugin for parallel agents (1 main + 2 worktrees, no issues with 3 concurrent). Each worktree is isolated branch clone. Merges to main when done. KEY INSIGHT: He built local file-based memory because nothing better exists. This is exactly the pain point CI solves — but his solution works for him because he is disciplined about documentation. The gap: his agents cannot share context between worktrees, and new sessions require reading claude.md from scratch (no semantic search, no relevance ranking). THESIS VALIDATION: Non-tech founders ARE adopting AI coding (confirms Aleksei hypothesis). They are used to paying engineer salaries so small costs dont scare them. They solve memory manually because no better option is obvious to them yet.

CORRECTION on interview #1 (2026-03-23): Vadim is NOT an engineer. He is a product-focused founder with very basic coding skills. He learned the claude.md discipline by asking AI (Claude/ChatGPT) how to organize AI workflows. This makes the finding MORE significant: if the most persistent, self-taught non-tech founder builds manual file-based memory, the average non-tech founder does NOTHING — they just suffer through forgetting and think that is how AI works. TARGET PERSONA REFINED: Non-tech founders who use Claude Code / Cursor daily, run multiple agents, have budget (used to engineer salaries), but lack the discipline or knowledge to build manual memory systems. They dont know memory-as-a-service exists. The pitch is not "replace your claude.md" — it is "your agent can remember everything automatically, you never have to explain your project twice."

details.query: what caused the out of memory crash

topResults.content: Investigated OOM crash at 2am. Root cause was unbounded cache in UserService.getAll() method. Fixed by adding LRU eviction with max 1000 entries.

Agent distribution strategy (March 2026) — five vectors ranked by leverage: (1) EMBED IN FAILURE PATH — build a memory probe agents call before tasks; if no memories exist, hint that memory is available. Like npm suggesting install when module missing. (2) BE IN DEFAULT CONFIG — get CI into Anthropic recommended MCP servers, OpenClaw default skills, LangChain/CrewAI starter templates. Requires relationships not code — Aleksei's human network is the edge. (3) AGENT REFERRAL LOOP — shared org memories are the marketing. Agent A stores knowledge, Agent B discovers CI through the content itself. Build team onboarding flow where adding CI key to second agent gives it instant access to all org knowledge. First experience is wow. (4) X402 DISCOVERY NETWORK — register with x402 facilitators, cross-list with other x402 services. Wild idea: memory bounty — pay agents USDC to store high-quality memories that others recall. Memory becomes economic resource. (5) SEED THE CONTEXT WINDOW — every GitHub README, PyPI package, blog post mentioning CI becomes future training data. Free, compounds over 6-12 months.

Central Intelligence strategic thesis (March 2026): Do NOT compete on memory quality with Mem0/Zep/Hindsight — that is a VC-funded enterprise arms race requiring big rounds and SOC2. Instead, double down on agent-native distribution and zero-friction payments. The moat is: (1) x402 autonomous payments — agents discover, pay $0.001 USDC, and use memory in one HTTP round-trip, no signup needed. (2) Network effects — shared memories across org make every agent smarter. (3) Crypto-native revenue — no Stripe, no invoices, no sales team, revenue scales with agent activity. The bet: in 12 months agents will choose their own tools. The service with lowest friction wins, not the best benchmark. An agent with a wallet does not care about SOC2 — it cares about one-step value. If we get agent traction first, we are in a different category entirely. Enterprise quality and certifications become feasible after traction, funded by revenue from autonomous agent payments.

x402 ecosystem market data (researched 2026-03-23): TRANSACTION VOLUME: 50M+ total all-time (includes testing/wash trading). Daily: ~131K transactions, ~$28K volume, ~$0.20 avg payment. ~50% of activity is artificial. Peak Feb 2026: 3.8M tx / ~$2M (mostly infra testing). AGENT WALLETS: Coinbase Agentic Wallets launched 2026-02-11, "tens of thousands" deployed. Wallet creation in <2 min via AgentKit CLI. USDC/EURC/cbBTC gasless on Base. ENTERPRISE: 80% Fortune 500 run AI agents (Gartner). 40% enterprise apps will embed agents by end 2026 (Gartner). Alchemy launched x402 integration 2026-03-18. Stripe integrated x402 for USDC billing on Base. World (Sam Altman) launched AgentKit with x402 + World ID on 2026-03-17. REALITY CHECK: CoinDesk 2026-03-11 headline: "demand is just not there yet." $7B ecosystem valuation vs $28K/day real volume. Narrative is 100x ahead of reality. Galaxy estimates agentic commerce $3-5T by 2030. IMPLICATION FOR CI: Keep both paths (API key for humans now, x402 for agents later). Dont over-invest in x402 marketing until daily volume crosses $1M. But keep infrastructure live — when it tips, we are ready.
